name: Community Bank Of Cameron
description_short: Community Bank of Cameron is a local Wisconsin bank offering checking, savings, mortgages, and retirement accounts across three branch locations with 24/7 online and mobile banking.
description_long: Community Bank of Cameron is a regional bank operating in Wisconsin with a primary focus on serving local communities through traditional banking services. The bank maintains three physical branch locations in Cameron, Grantsburg, and Siren, Wisconsin, and has been actively involved in community engagement and leadership development. The bank emphasizes accessibility and modernization, as evidenced by their AudioEye-enabled website and mobile app with travel notification features. Community Bank of Cameron offers a comprehensive range of financial products including checking and savings accounts (including their "Fun Money Account" for savings goals), mortgage lending and refinancing services, retirement planning through IRAs, loan products, and investment planning services. The bank distinguishes itself through local decision-making, with named leadership (Bank President Ted Gerber) and a community-focused approach including charitable giving programs such as their Community Cares Grants. They actively promote financial education through seminars, calculators, and an online education center, and celebrate employee tenure and customer relationships. A notable caveat is that as a smaller regional bank with limited branch presence, Community Bank of Cameron may have fewer physical locations and potentially less extensive product offerings compared to larger national banking institutions. Their service area appears concentrated in northwest Wisconsin (Polk County region), which may limit accessibility for customers outside this geographic area. The bank maintains standard regulatory compliance as a Member FDIC and Equal Housing Lender. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Loan products (general loans and specialized loans)
Debit cards with travel notification features
Online education center and financial seminars
pros: 24/7 online and mobile banking with travel notification features for debit cards
Three convenient branch locations in Cameron, Grantsburg, and Siren with extended drive-up hours (8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. weekdays, 8:30 a.m. to 11:00 a.m. Saturdays)
Specialized savings product (Fun Money Account) designed for specific savings goals
Free financial education seminars on topics like estate planning and retirement planning
Active community engagement including charitable Community Cares Grants program
AudioEye-enabled website with accessibility optimization features
FDIC Member and Equal Housing Lender with established routing number (091804723)
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to three Wisconsin locations, reducing accessibility for customers outside the immediate area
Smaller institution likely means fewer specialized financial products compared to regional or national banks
No mention of competitive rates or rate comparison tools on website to assess pricing competitiveness
Limited ATM network with only two mentioned ATM locations (Cameron and Grantsburg), potentially inconvenient for frequent cash access
Lobby hours are relatively limited (9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. weekdays only), restricting in-person service availability for working customers
best_for: Customers in northwest Wisconsin (Polk County area) seeking convenient local banking with personal relationships and community involvement
Individuals interested in mortgages and refinancing who prefer working with local loan officers rather than large national lenders
Customers seeking financial education and planning services through personalized local guidance for IRAs and retirement planning
diagnosis: Community Bank of Cameron is best suited for customers in northwest Wisconsin seeking traditional banking services with local decision-making, personalized service, and community engagement. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—customers outside Polk County Wisconsin will find the three-branch footprint restrictive, and the institution's smaller size means it may not offer the specialized products or competitive rates of larger regional or national banks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
